rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cascade synthesis of five- and six-membered thiazolidine-2-imines via C–S/C–S(Se) bond formation from isothiocyanates, N-2-en-1-amines, and KSCN/KSeCN
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of SCN- (or SeCN-) anion to thiocyanyl radical (and its dimer (SCN)2); cathodic reduction of protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: N-alkylurea (unsaturated N-allyl thiourea III, from amine addition to isothiocyanate)
Carbon-centered radical IV (thiocyanyl-added to olefin of III)
Cationic intermediate V (after anodic SET of IV)
Cationic intermediate VI (from III + (SCN)2, ionic path b)

electron_flow: Amine adds to isothiocyanate to form N-allyl thiourea III; at the anode, SCN- is oxidized to SCN• (and dimerizes to (SCN)2). Path a: SCN• adds to the pendant olefin of III to give C-radical IV, which is further anodically oxidized to cation V and cyclized by intramolecular S-attack with deprotonation to product VII. Path b: (SCN)2 acts as electrophile on III to give cation VI plus SCN-, then cyclization. Cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 12%, indicating involvement of radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 31%, supporting radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Cinnamyl-type aryl-substituted N-2-en-1-amine (42)
probe_reagents.observation: Six-membered thiazolidine-2-imine 43 obtained in 76% yield, consistent with stabilized benzylic radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Isolated N-alkylurea 41
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated in 92% under standard conditions in 5 min, confirming initial amine + isothiocyanate addition

probe_reagents.name: KSCN (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 1.40 V vs Ag/AgCl, attributed to SCN- → SCN• then dimerization to (SCN)2; thiourea 41 showed no oxidation peak

notes: Authors propose two parallel pathways: a radical path a (SCN• addition then anodic oxidation to cation) and an ionic path b ((SCN)2 acting as electrophile). Both are stated to operate; favored description in scheme starts with path a. Reaction is metal-, oxidant-, and electrolyte-free, undivided cell with graphite electrodes.
